1: A direct revelation: 'The LORD says to my Lord: Sit at my right hand until I make your enemies your footstool.' The most foundational Christological oracle in the OT: the risen and ascended Christ is enthroned at the Father's right hand (the supreme position of divine authority) awaiting the final subjection of all enemies, including death (1 Corinthians 15:25-26). The present era is the era of Christ's enthronement: he reigns now, his enemies are being progressively placed under his feet, and the ultimate subjection is certain. Psalms 110:1 frames the entire NT's understanding of the ascension and the present Christological situation of the cosmos.
4: A fulfillment: 'You are a priest forever after the order of Melchizedek.' The divine oath of the eternal priesthood is fulfilled in Christ's resurrection, which established him as the priest who never dies. Unlike the Levitical priests who needed to offer sacrifice repeatedly and who were replaced by death, Christ offered himself once and lives forever in the power of an indestructible life (Hebrews 7:16, 24-25). The Melchizedekian priesthood — prior to Aaron, not from the tribe of Levi, without recorded end — is the OT's own signal that the Levitical system was provisional. Christ's eternal intercession ('he always lives to make intercession for them', Hebrews 7:25) is the fulfillment of Psalms 110:4's oath.
2: direct The LORD sends forth your mighty scepter from Zion. Rule in the midst of your enemies! The ruling scepter sent from Zion — Christ's current reign from his heavenly enthronement. Acts 2:33-36: God has made Jesus both Lord and Christ, seated at his right hand. All authority in heaven and on earth has been given to me (Matthew 28:18). He rules now, in the midst of enemies, through the church's proclamation and the Spirit's power — the mission of Matthew 28:19 is the exercise of this scepter.
3: shadow Your people will offer themselves freely on the day of your power, in holy garments; from the womb of the morning, the dew of your youth will be like dew. Freely offered, holy-garmented people on the day of power — the church gathered by the Spirit at Pentecost. God is at work in you, both to will and to work for his good pleasure (Philippians 2:13). Holy garments: clothed with Christ (Galatians 3:27). The dew of youth: the inexhaustible vitality of Christ's eternal priesthood, the endless spring of the new covenant people.
5: shadow The Lord is at your right hand; he will shatter kings on the day of his wrath. Shattering kings on the day of wrath — Revelation 19:11-16: the rider on the white horse, with a name written King of kings and Lord of lords, strikes down the nations. The day of Christ's wrath is the day of his return; the kings who resisted his rule face the scepter that shatters (Psalms 2:9: you shall break them with a rod of iron, and dash them in pieces like a potter's vessel).
6: shadow He will execute judgment among the nations, filling them with corpses; he will shatter chiefs over the wide earth. Judgment executed among the nations — the final judgment of Revelation 20:11-15 and Revelation 19:17-21. Matthew 25:31-46: all nations gathered before the Son of Man; he separates the sheep from the goats, executing judgment across the wide earth. The one who came first in humility comes again in the fullness of this authority.
7: shadow He will drink from the brook by the way; therefore he will lift up his head. Drinking from the brook and lifting up the head — the humiliation and exaltation of Christ. The warrior-king pauses to drink (condescension into mortality); then lifts his head in victory. Christ took the cup of suffering (Matthew 26:42: let this cup not pass from me), drank it fully on the cross, and was exalted: Therefore God has highly exalted him (Philippians 2:9). Hebrews 12:2: who for the joy set before him endured the cross... and is seated at the right hand of the throne of God.
